sanskrit:
ब्राह्मणक्षत्रियविशां शूद्राणां च परन्तप।
कर्माणि प्रविभक्तानि स्वभावप्रभवैर्गुणैः॥
transliteration:
brāhmaṇa-kṣatriya-viśāṃ śūdrāṇāṃ ca parantapa
karmāṇi pravibhaktāni svabhāva-prabhavair guṇaiḥ
literal_meaning: The actions of brāhmaṇas, kṣatriyas, vaiśyas, and śūdras, O scorcher of foes, are divided according to the qualities born of their own nature.
contextual_meaning: Human identity is not fixed by birth but expressed through innate psychological tendencies (guṇas) that shape vocational calling and moral duty.
psychological_state: Post-decision integration—Arjuna now sees social roles as expressions of temperament rather than rigid labels, reducing identity anxiety.
trigger_scenario: A software engineer realizes she thrives in mentoring (brāhmaṇa-guṇa) more than coding, and renegotiates her job description instead of quitting.
anchor_text: Your work is the natural flowering of your inner nature, not a label stamped at birth.
